name: Nexalin Technology, Inc.
business: Nexalin designs and develops innovative neurostimulation products to treat mental health conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and depression. The company generates revenue from monthly licensing fees and sales of electrodes and patient cables for its Gen-1 devices, which are still in service, and from international sales of its Gen-2 devices. Marketing of new Gen-1 devices in the U.S. has been paused following the FDA's December 2019 reclassification.

exposures.sentence: We design and develop innovative neurostimulation products to uniquely and effectively help combat the ongoing global mental health epidemic.
We have now completed our research and have begun developing our virtual clinic on a digital platform with a leading IT design team.

catalysts.event: FDA accepted Q-Submission for Gen-2 SYNC system for Alzheimer's treatment
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the FDA formally accepted our Q-Submission (Q-Sub) related to the Company's Gen-2 Console (SYNC) system for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease and dementia.

catalysts.event: Company met with FDA to discuss Alzheimer's strategy
catalysts.sentence: The Company met with the FDA in November 2025 and continues to have discussions to develop a strategy and protocol.

catalysts.event: Second FDA pre-submission document for Gen-3 HALO submitted
catalysts.sentence: A second FDA pre-submission document was submitted on February 13, 2024.

catalysts.event: FDA teleconference on Gen-3 HALO pre-submission
catalysts.sentence: A formal teleconference was held with the FDA on April 26, 2024.

catalysts.event: Brazilian regulatory approval for Gen-2 device
catalysts.sentence: On June 13, 2024, the Company announced that our Gen-2 device had been granted regulatory approval by the Brazilian Health Regulatory Agency.
In January 2026, we established a wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WOFE) in the People's Republic of China.

catalysts.event: Short-term agreement with Lindus Health for clinical trial preparation
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the Company entered into a short-term start-up agreement with Lindus Health Limited to support limited clinical trial preparation activities while a broader services agreement is under negotiation.

tamClaims.claim: Insomnia responsible for at least $63 billion in direct and indirect healthcare costs annually
tamClaims.sentence: Insomnia is a common sleep disorder considered to be responsible for at least $63 billion in direct and indirect healthcare costs each year, according to the Harvard American Insomnia Study.

tamClaims.claim: Substance use disorders cost U.S. more than $740 billion a year
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Institute on Drug Abuse (NIDA) substance use, and substance use disorders cost the United States more than $740 billion a year in healthcare, crime and lost productivity costs;

tamClaims.claim: Global chronic pain treatment market projected to generate revenue of $173 billion in 2033
tamClaims.sentence: According to Research and Markets, the global chronic pain treatment market is predicted to progress at a CAGR of 7% and generate revenue of $173 billion in 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Global Alzheimer's therapeutics market projected to reach $13.7 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to Reports and Data, the global Alzheimer's therapeutics market is projected to reach $13.7 billion by 2030 from $2.2 billion in 2020 with a substantial compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 20% through the forecast period.
